name: The 'Layering Override Group' Slots are a great place to play additional upper body animations, like reloads, equips, etc. By using a single montage, you can apply an animation in different ways on different body parts by using the different slots. For example, you could play a reload animation on the arms, and play an additive version of the reload on the body so that it can be used in any stance. Just remember to include the 'Curves' slot so you can control how the curves get blended in.
title: The 'Layering Override Group' Slots are a great place to play additional upper body animations, like reloads, equips, etc. By using a single montage, you can apply an animation in different ways on different body parts by using the different slots. For example, you could play a reload animation on the arms, and play an additive version of the reload on the body so that it can be used in any stance. Just remember to include the 'Curves' slot so you can control how the curves get blended in.

name: Play a normal or additive anim in the 'Curves' slot when playing a Layering Override animation to override or influence the layering curve values.
title: Play a normal or additive anim in the 'Curves' slot when playing a Layering Override animation to override or influence the layering curve values.
terms: Play a normal or additive anim in the 'Curves' slot when playing a Layering Override animation to override or influence the layering curve values.

name: Both a mesh space and local space version of the Base Additive is created so that different body parts can recieve different additive types. Local space works best on the arms so that the motion respects the rotation induced by the spine.
title: Both a mesh space and local space version of the Base Additive is created so that different body parts can recieve different additive types. Local space works best on the arms so that the motion respects the rotation induced by the spine.

name: The extra 'Hands' layer is used to prevent additive motion on the arms from playing on the fingers and ik gun / object bones if the character is holding something.
title: The extra 'Hands' layer is used to prevent additive motion on the arms from playing on the fingers and ik gun / object bones if the character is holding something.

name: Create layers from different body sections.
title: Create layers from different body sections.
terms: Create layers from different body sections.

name: Although these layer nodes have no bones, they still influences the curve values. This allows the system to take the values directly from the curves present in the Base and Overlay Layer, preventing them from being affected by all the previous layering. If this is not done, it would be nearly impossible to control how the curves interact with each other, since they drive the blend amounts in the layering process.
title: Although these layer nodes have no bones, they still influences the curve values. This allows the system to take the values directly from the curves present in the Base and Overlay Layer, preventing them from being affected by all the previous layering. If this is not done, it would be nearly impossible to control how the curves interact with each other, since they drive the blend amounts in the layering process.

name: Interp Foot IK offsets back to 0
title: Interp Foot IK offsets back to 0
terms: Interp Foot IK offsets back to 0

name: Step 1: Check if the character should rotate left or right by checking if the Aiming Angle exceeds the threshold.
title: Step 1: Check if the character should rotate left or right by checking if the Aiming Angle exceeds the threshold.
terms: Step 1: Check if the character should rotate left or right by checking if the Aiming Angle exceeds the threshold.

name: Step 2: If the character should be rotating, set the Rotate Rate to scale with the Aim Yaw Rate. This makes the character rotate faster when moving the camera faster.
title: Step 2: If the character should be rotating, set the Rotate Rate to scale with the Aim Yaw Rate. This makes the character rotate faster when moving the camera faster.

name: Only update values if FootIK curve has a weight.
title: Only update values if FootIK curve has a weight.
terms: Only update values if FootIK curve has a weight.

name: Step 1: Set Local FootLock Curve value
title: Step 1: Set Local FootLock Curve value
terms: Step 1: Set Local FootLock Curve value

name: Step 2: Only update the FootLock Alpha if the new value is less than the current, or it equals 1. This makes it so that the foot can only blend out of the locked position or lock to a new position, and never blend in.
title: Step 2: Only update the FootLock Alpha if the new value is less than the current, or it equals 1. This makes it so that the foot can only blend out of the locked position or lock to a new position, and never blend in.

name: Step 3: If the Foot Lock curve equals 1, save the new lock location and rotation in component space.
title: Step 3: If the Foot Lock curve equals 1, save the new lock location and rotation in component space.
terms: Step 3: If the Foot Lock curve equals 1, save the new lock location and rotation in component space.

name: Step 4: If the Foot Lock Alpha has a weight, update the Foot Lock offsets to keep the foot planted in place while the capsule moves.
title: Step 4: If the Foot Lock Alpha has a weight, update the Foot Lock offsets to keep the foot planted in place while the capsule moves.
terms: Step 4: If the Foot Lock Alpha has a weight, update the Foot Lock offsets to keep the foot planted in place while the capsule moves.

name: Use the delta between the current and last updated rotation to find how much the foot should be rotated to remain planted on the ground.
title: Use the delta between the current and last updated rotation to find how much the foot should be rotated to remain planted on the ground.
terms: Use the delta between the current and last updated rotation to find how much the foot should be rotated to remain planted on the ground.

name: Get the distance traveled between frames relative to the mesh rotation to find how much the foot should be offset to remain planted on the ground.
title: Get the distance traveled between frames relative to the mesh rotation to find how much the foot should be offset to remain planted on the ground.
terms: Get the distance traveled between frames relative to the mesh rotation to find how much the foot should be offset to remain planted on the ground.

name: Subtract the location difference from the current local location and rotate it by the rotation difference to keep the foot planted in component space.
title: Subtract the location difference from the current local location and rotate it by the rotation difference to keep the foot planted in component space.
terms: Subtract the location difference from the current local location and rotate it by the rotation difference to keep the foot planted in component space.

name: Subtract the Rotation Difference from the current Local Rotation to get the new local rotation.
title: Subtract the Rotation Difference from the current Local Rotation to get the new local rotation.
terms: Subtract the Rotation Difference from the current Local Rotation to get the new local rotation.
